hero.tagline: Moroccan authenticity, delivered to your door
hero.description: The flavors, care and soul of Morocco — shaped by its women, delivered intact to you

about.label: About us
about.heading: Our Story
about.quote: "The soul of Morocco, shaped by its women, delivered intact to you"
about.text: Biohna is a platform that directly connects Moroccan women artisans with consumers seeking authenticity. Every product is 100% natural, handmade, and tells a story.
about.mission: Our mission: to value ancestral know-how while providing a fair and dignified source of income for these exceptional women.

family.label: The Biohna Family
family.heading: Here for you.
family.subheading: Close to you, everywhere in Morocco
family.join_btn: Become a member of the BIOHNA FAMILY

cooperatives_items.name: Aït Baha Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Founded in 2003 by 34 Berber women, Aït Baha is the world reference for cosmetic argan oil. Their cold pressing preserves all the properties of Morocco's liquid gold.

cooperatives_items.name: Tifawin Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Tifawin ('light' in Tamazight) brings together 60 artisans specializing in natural cosmetics. Their beldi soap, cooked over fig wood, is renowned for its exceptional purifying properties.

cooperatives_items.name: Dar Lalla Fatima
cooperatives_items.bio: Since 1998, Lalla Fatima unites the beekeepers of the Middle Atlas plains. Their wild thyme raw honey is harvested twice a year according to lunar cycles.

cooperatives_items.name: Amalou Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Fifteen women from the Souss valley perpetuate the ancestral recipe for amlou — the almond, argan and honey paste that Berbers offer to brides. Each jar is stone-ground.

cooperatives_items.name: Zitoun Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Nestled in the palm groves of Marrakech, Zitoun transforms ancient olive varieties into extra virgin oils of rare aromatic richness. Extraction on the day of harvest.

cooperatives_items.name: Zaafrane Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Taliouine produces 95% of Moroccan saffron. The 80 women of Zaafrane harvest the pistils by hand, before dawn, during the three weeks of annual flowering.

cooperatives_items.name: Hanane Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: In the medina of Fès, Hanane perpetuates the art of hand-rolled couscous. Each grain of semolina is worked with gestures passed from mother to daughter for seven generations.

cooperatives_items.name: Amassine Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: At the heart of the desert, Amassine grows Medjool and Boufeggous dates in the oasis palm groves. Natural sun-drying and packaging without preservatives.

cooperatives_items.name: Tamazight Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: The weavers of Azilal create Berber carpets with a unique geometric motif — the textile zellige. Atlas sheep wool, dyed with madder and henna.

cooperatives_items.name: High Atlas Argan Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: At 1,800m altitude, these women produce a mountain argan with a more intense flavor. The rarity of the altitude gives their culinary oil an incomparable toasted note.

cooperatives_items.name: Nour Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Nour brings together the herbalists of the Rif. Their expertise in steam distillation produces floral waters — Damascus rose, orange blossom, lavender — of incomparable purity.

cooperatives_items.name: Belhda Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: In the blue city, Belhda produces fresh goat cheese with Rif herbs. Free-range farming, 100% natural diet, aged in a stone cellar.

cooperatives_items.name: Laârouss Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: On the windswept Atlantic coast, Laârouss cultivates wild capers and seaweed. Their iodine-rich condiments bring the taste of the Moroccan Atlantic to your kitchen.

cooperatives_items.name: Drâa Weaving Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Along the Drâa valley, 45 women weave organic cotton haïks and gandouras. Their creations blend nomadic motifs and contemporary sobriety.

cooperatives_items.name: Ghassoul Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: The only cooperative that extracts raw ghassoul in the world. This volcanic clay from the Middle Atlas cleanses and mineralizes skin and hair without chemical detergents.

cooperatives_items.name: Agdal Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Under the cedars of the Middle Atlas, Agdal raises honey bees in traditional cork hives. Their cedar honey is the rarest and most prized in Morocco.

cooperatives_items.name: Amazigh Beauty Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Amazigh Beauty formulates facial care from plants endemic to the High Atlas: argania, euphorbia, wild argan. No preservatives, 100% locavore formulation.

cooperatives_items.name: Tagnaoute Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: Specializing in central Moroccan spices, Tagnaoute produces ras el hanout according to a secret 27-plant composition. Stone-ground on the day of your order.

cooperatives_items.name: Arfoud Dates Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: In the oases of Tafilalet, Arfoud produces Boufeggous and Jihel dates sun-dried in the desert. Their fermented date syrup is a treasure of Saharan cuisine.

cooperatives_items.name: Timghoula Cooperative
cooperatives_items.bio: At the junction of the Middle and High Atlas, Timghoula cultivates ancient apple varieties and transforms their juice into natural apple cider vinegar, fermented six months in glass.

footer.copyright: © 2024 BIOHNA. MOROCCAN AUTHENTICITY, DELIVERED TO YOUR DOOR.
footer.brand_desc: Cultivating authentic Moroccan craft through women artisans, slow production, and uncompromising quality.
